summary: This is a very brief news update post where Joel Spolsky celebrates his book 'The Best Software Writing I' reaching #1 in computer books on Amazon and selling out its first printing in three days. He humorously attributes part of its success to including a contributor's drawing of a cow. He also shares a quote from Queen Elizabeth II complaining about Sony's overly complex remote controls, endorsing her sentiment about poor UI design.
key_points: Joel's curated anthology 'The Best Software Writing I' hit #1 on Amazon's computer books list and sold out its first printing in three days
Humor and personality (like a contributor's cow drawing) can differentiate a tech book from dry competitors
Even Queen Elizabeth II finds Sony's remote controls too complex, illustrating that bad UI design is universally frustrating
money_quotes: Apparently it is completely whuping "PMP Exam Prep (4th Edition)", which is number 2.
They probably didn't think to put Leon Bambrick's drawing of a cow in their book.
I have a lot of trouble with your remote controls. Too many arrows.
key_insight: Good UI design is so universally important that even a queen will call out needlessly complex interfaces.
